sect1.title: OBS-Studio-&obs-studio-version;
sect1.sect2:
Introduction to OBS Studio
OBS Studio is a screen recorder that can be piped to various media containers and remote servers. It is geared towards streaming but also has a very much supported general recording usecase.
&long-build-time;
Download (HTTP):
Additional Downloads
Required patch:
Recommended download:
OBS Studio Dependencies
Required
alsa-plugins, CMake, cURL, &ecm;, FFmpeg, FreeType2, Fontconfig, GLib (with GObject Introspection), Jansson, libdrm, libva, libxkbcommon, , &opengl;, pciutils, Qt-6,
, Vulkan-Loader, Xorg Libraries, and x264
Recommended
, , and
(required for WebSocket support)
at-spi2-core and Cups (required for browser support)
&cuda; and nv-codec-headers (required for NVIDIA hardware encoding)
fdk-aac (required for FDK AAC codec support)
intel-media-driver, (for GPUs from Broadwell to Tiger Lake), (for GPUs from Tiger Lake onward), , and Mesa (required for Intel QSV11 hardware encoding)
(required for WebRTC support)
Pipewire and xdg-desktop-portal (required for Pipewire camera support and screencasting)
PulseAudio (required for PulseAudio audio sources)
Speex (required for SpeexDSP noise suppression)
SWIG and luajit (required for scripting support)
v4l-utils (required for V4L support)
VLC (required for VLC support)
&egl; and Wayland (required for Wayland support)
The options to disable the functionality enabled by the dependencies above are in the Command Explanations.
Optional
Jack2, libajantv2, librist, librnnoise (if not installed, an internal copy will be used), and sndio

sect2.title: Installation of OBS Studio
sect2.para: Apply a patch to allow building with Qt-6.10.x, above, and below:

important.para: If you are on Qt-6.10.x, ensure that it was built with the upstream fixes patch. If it wasn't, any package including this one will crash when using qtdeclarative. This should get fixed in Qt-6.11, which is in beta currently.

sect2.para: If FFmpeg is 8.0 or above, allow the code to build against it:

screen.userinput: if pkgconf --atleast-version 62 libavformat; then sed -i 's/FF_/AV_/g' \ plugins/obs-ffmpeg/obs-ffmpeg-vaapi.c fi

sect2.para: If you want to enable browser support, ensure you have downloaded the recommended download, then unpack it:
Install OBS Studio by running the following commands:

screen.userinput:
mkdir build && cd build &&
cmake -D CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr \ -D CMAKE_SKIP_INSTALL_RPATH=ON \ -D OBS_VERSION_OVERRIDE="&obs-studio-version;" \ -D ENABLE_BROWSER=ON \ -D CEF_ROOT_DIR=../cef-&obs-cef-version; \ -D ENABLE_AJA=OFF \ -D ENABLE_JACK=OFF \ -D ENABLE_NEW_MPEGTS_OUTPUT=OFF \ -D OBS_COMPILE_DEPRECATION_AS_WARNING=ON \ -G Ninja -Wno-dev .. &&
ninja

note.para: Normally for Meson and CMake projects, the build type is set to release. However, doing so for OBS-Studio will cause it to have a segmentation fault as CMake uses Interprocedural Optimization, or IPO, which conflicts with the set build type in unexpected ways. The above setup lets CMake figure it out as to not introduce conflicts.

sect1.sect2:
Command Explanations
&cmake-skip-install-rpath;
-D OBS_VERSION_OVERRIDE="&obs-studio-version;": This switch sets the OBS-Studio version properly, which is otherwise set at 0.
-D ENABLE_BROWSER=ON: This switch enables browser support.
-D CEF_ROOT_DIR=../cef-&obs-cef-version;: This switch tells the build system where the CEF binaries are located.
-D ENABLE_AJA=OFF: This switch disables the optional Aja functionality.
-D ENABLE_JACK=OFF: This switch disables the optional Jack2 functionality.
-D ENABLE_NEW_MPEGTS_OUTPUT=OFF: This switch disables the optional MpegTS Output functionality.
-D ENABLE_PIPEWIRE=OFF: Disables Pipewire support if you have not installed it.
-D ENABLE_SPEEXDSP=OFF: Disables Speex support if you have not installed it.
-D ENABLE_WEBRTC=OFF: Disables WebRTC support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_WEBSOCKET=OFF: Disables WebSocket support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_V4L2=OFF: Disables V4L2 support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_VLC=OFF: Disables VLC support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_NVENC=OFF -D ENABLE_NVENC_FFMPEG_IDS=OFF: These options disable NVIDIA hardware encoding support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_QSV11=OFF: Disables Intel QSV11 hardware encoding support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D ENABLE_SCRIPTING=OFF: Disables scripting support if you have not installed the recommended dependencies.
-D OBS_COMPILE_DEPRECATION_AS_WARNING=ON: This ensures there are no build failures due to deprecated features in the FFmpeg side being used.

seglistitem.seg: libobs-frontend-api, libobs-opengl, libobs-scripting, libobs, and many plugins in /usr/lib/obs-plugins

listitem.para: is the FFmpeg multiplexer that obs uses
contains API functions for the OBS Studio frontend
provided OpenGL rendering functions for OBS Studio
provides functions for OBS Studio scripts
contains all the functions used by OBS Studio and its plugins
